updateMessage: Adds experimental Spain 2026 resident tax rules, including IRPF approximations, Seguridad Social payroll deductions, and Spanish fund / ETF wrappers.

incomeTax.name: IRPF
incomeTax.tooltip: Resident Spain IRPF approximation using a national baseline schedule plus optional autonomous-community profiles via the regional selector. The EUR 5,550 personal minimum and EUR 2,000 employment deduction are modeled as pre-bracket adjustments.

regionalIncomeTax.name: IRPF autonómico
regionalIncomeTax.tooltip: Autonomous-community regional IRPF component selectable through country inputs. The default profile keeps regional tax neutral when no profile is selected.

socialContributions.name: Cotizaciones a la Seguridad Social
socialContributions.tooltip: Grouped employee-side 2026 general-regime contribution bucket for common contingencies (4.70%), unemployment on standard indefinite contracts (1.55%), training (0.10%), and the intergenerational-equity mechanism (0.15%), capped at the 2026 monthly maximum contribution base annualized by FinSim. Fixed-term contract differences, daily payroll rounding, and special regimes are documented in the gap report.

additionalTaxes.name: Cotización de solidaridad
additionalTaxes.tooltip: Additional 2026 employee solidarity contribution on salary above the general-regime maximum contribution base, annualized from the official monthly tranches of EUR 5,101.21-5,611.32, EUR 5,611.33-7,651.80, and over EUR 7,651.80.

capitalGainsTax.name: Ganancias del ahorro
capitalGainsTax.tooltip: Spanish savings-base rate schedule for gains on asset disposals. FinSim uses the savings-rate ladder directly for modeled realizations and does not model the separate dividend / interest netting baskets, pre-1994 transitional abatements, or other special savings-base rules.

helpText.label: Planes de pensiones
helpText.contributionDescriptionP1:
Enter how much of the modeled Spanish annual tax-reduction limit you want to contribute from salary into a pension plan.

FinSim uses the standard EUR 1,500 resident individual limit and does not model the higher employer-linked reduction paths.
helpText.contributionDescriptionP2:
Enter how much of your partner's modeled Spanish annual tax-reduction limit you want to contribute from salary into a pension plan.

FinSim uses the same standard EUR 1,500 resident individual limit for both people.
helpText.statePensionDescriptionP1:
Enter your expected monthly public-pension amount.

Use your Seguridad Social estimate or other retirement statement.
helpText.statePensionDescriptionP2:
Enter your partner's expected monthly public-pension amount.

Use their Seguridad Social estimate or other retirement statement.

investmentTypes.label: Acciones cotizadas
investmentTypes.helpText: Direct listed shares are modeled under Spain's savings-income capital-gains schedule of 19% to 30%. Pre-1994 transitional abatements, dividend withholding detail, and cross-category savings-base offsets are not modeled.

investmentTypes.label: Fondos de inversión
investmentTypes.helpText: Spanish / EU investment funds are modeled under the same savings-income gains schedule as other taxable investments. Real tax-free traspasos between eligible funds are not modeled, so FinSim taxes sales that Spanish law may defer.

investmentTypes.label: ETFs
investmentTypes.helpText: ETFs are modeled under Spain's savings-income gains schedule of 19% to 30%. Unlike eligible mutual-fund traspasos, ETF disposals are treated as taxable sales in both real Spanish law and FinSim.

investmentTypes.label: RSUs
investmentTypes.helpText: Employee share awards are generally taxed as employment income when they vest or are granted, with later sale gains falling into the savings-income schedule. Spain's employee-share exemptions, startup-law deferral, and stock-option edge cases are not modeled.
